revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle. Moved one year back to resolve earlier import errors.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Calendrical Notes]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''暦注'''とは、[[暦]]に記載される日時・方角などの吉凶、その日の運勢などの事項のことである。
'''暦注'''の大半は、[[陰陽五行説]]、[[十干十二支]]に基づいたものである。一般に、暦の上段には日付・[[曜日]]・[[二十四節季]]などの科学的・天文学的な事項が書かれ、中段には[[十二直]]、下段には[[選日]]・[[二十八宿]]・[[九星]]などの非科学的・迷信的な事項が書かれる。この中・下段の記載事項を暦注と呼ぶ。また、[[六曜]]は日付の下に書かれることが多いが、これも暦注に入れる。

{{出典の明記|date=2020年1月}} '''暦注'''（れきちゅう）とは、[[暦]]に記載される日時・[[方位]]などの吉凶、その日の運勢などの事項のことである。
== 概要 == 暦注の大半は、[[陰陽五行説]]、[[十干]][[十二支]]（[[干支]]）に基づいたものである。一般に、暦の上段には日付・[[曜日]]・[[二十四節気]]、[[七十二候]]などの科学的・天文学的な事項や年中行事が書かれ、中段には[[十二直]]、下段には[[選日]]・[[二十八宿]]・[[九星]]・[[暦注下段]]などの事項が書かれる。<!--この中・下段の記載事項を暦注と呼ぶ。-->また、[[六曜]]は日付の下に書かれることが多いが、これも暦注に入れる。

{{Uranai-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:れきちゆう}} [[Category:暦法]] [[Category:占星術]] [[Category:占い]] [[Category:術数学]] [[Category:暦注|*]] [[Category:陰陽道]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年1月}} '''暦注'''（れきちゅう）とは、[[暦]]に記載される日時・[[方位]]などの[[吉凶]]、その日の[[運|運勢]]などの事項のことである。
== 概要 == 暦注の大半は、[[陰陽五行説]]、[[十干]][[十二支]]（[[干支]]）に基づいたものである。一般に、暦の上段には日付・[[曜日]]・[[二十四節気]]、[[七十二候]]などの科学的・天文学的な事項や[[年中行事]]が書かれ、中段には[[十二直]]、下段には[[選日]]・[[二十八宿]]・[[九星]]・[[暦注下段]]などの事項が書かれる。<!--この中・下段の記載事項を暦注と呼ぶ。-->また、[[六曜]]は日付の下に書かれることが多いが、これも暦注に入れる。
暦注の日取りを決める方法を[[撰日法]]という。
== 関連項目 == *[[カレンダー]] *[[雑書]] *[[生活暦]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?http://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.htm 暦注計算] - こよみのページ *{{kotobank}}

revision.text:
{{Cite need|date=January 2020}} '''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|calendar|ja|暦}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説}} and the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}
{{Uranai-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Fortune telling]] [[Category:Divination mathematics]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Rekichū]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bumping for category 8]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:暦注]]

== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説}} and the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}

{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Nine Stars of Divination|Nine Stars in Indian Astronomy|Nine Luminaries}} {{Cite source|date=December 2018}} {{color}} '''Nine Stars''' ({{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星}}, Kyūsei) is a form of [[folk belief]] originating from ancient [[China]], consisting of nine stars: '''First White''', '''Second Black''', '''Third Blue''', '''Fourth Green''', '''Fifth Yellow''', '''Sixth White''', '''Seventh Red''', '''Eighth White''', and '''Ninth Purple'''.
The term "Nine Stars" can refer to several systems, and the one described here is specifically referred to as the "Purple White Nine Stars" or "Seven Colored Stars."
== Origin == The Nine Stars system originates from the following {{ill|magic square|ja|魔方陣}}:
{| class="wikitable" |- |Four||Nine||Two |- |Three||Five||Seven |- |Eight||One||Six |}
In this magic square, the sum of any row, column, or diagonal equals 15. It uses the numbers 1 through 9 exactly once in a 3×3 grid. This configuration, called the '''Later Heaven Arrangement''' ({{ill|Later Heaven Arrangement|ja|後天定位盤}}), is the only one of its kind, excluding rotations and reflections. These numbers are paired with seven colors (white, black, blue, green, yellow, red, and purple), the {{ill|five elements|ja|五行思想}} ({{ill|wood|ja|木}}, {{ill|fire|ja|火}}, {{ill|earth|ja|土}}, {{ill|metal|ja|金}}, and {{ill|water|ja|水}}), the {{ill|ten heavenly stems|ja|十干}}, the {{ill|twelve earthly branches|ja|十二支}}, and the {{ill|eight trigrams|ja|八卦}} of {{ill|I Ching|ja|易経}}. When these numbers shift positions, interpretations are added to form the "Nine Stars."
According to legend, {{ill|Yu the Great|ja|禹}}, founder of the {{ill|Xia Dynasty|ja|夏王朝}}, conceived this magic square when he encountered a sacred turtle emerging from the {{ill|Luo River|ja|洛水}}. This magic square is called the "Writing of the Luo" ({{ill|Luo Writing|ja|洛書}}), one of the {{ill|River Chart and Luo Writing|ja|河図洛書}}.
== The Nine Stars == In Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, the following table associates the Nine Stars with the {{ill|five elements|ja|五行思想}}, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, {{ill|eight trigrams|ja|八卦}}, and colors, forming the '''Nine Stars Chart''' used to predict {{ill|fortune|ja|運勢}} and the {{ill|auspiciousness|ja|吉凶}} of directions based on a person's birth year.
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Reading ! Five Element ! Direction ! Eight Trigram ! Color |- ! {{ill|First White Water Star|ja|一白水星}} | Ippaku Suisei | Water | {{ill|North|ja|北}} | {{ill|Kan|ja|坎}} | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Second Black Earth Star|ja|二黒土星}} | Jikoku Dosei | Earth | Southwest | {{ill|Kun|ja|坤}} | style="background:black;color:white;"| Black |- ! {{ill|Third Blue Wood Star|ja|三碧木星}} | Sanpeki Mokusei | Wood | {{ill|East|ja|東}} | {{ill|Zhen|ja|震}} | style="background:blue;color:white;"| Blue |- ! {{ill|Fourth Green Wood Star|ja|四緑木星}} | Shiroku Mokusei | Wood | Southeast | {{ill|Xun|ja|巽}} | style="background:green;color:white;"| Green |- ! {{ill|Fifth Yellow Earth Star|ja|五黄土星}} | Goō Dosei | Earth | Center | {{ill|Tai Chi|ja|太極}} | style="background:yellow;"| Yellow |- ! {{ill|Sixth White Metal Star|ja|六白金星}} | Roppaku Kinsei | Metal | Northwest | {{ill|Qian|ja|乾}} | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Seventh Red Metal Star|ja|七赤金星}} | Shichiseki Kinsei | Metal | {{ill|West|ja|西}} | {{ill|Dui|ja|兌}} | style="background:red;color:white;"| Red |- ! {{ill|Eighth White Earth Star|ja|八白土星}} | Happaku Dosei | Earth | Northeast | {{ill|Gen|ja|艮}} | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Ninth Purple Fire Star|ja|九紫火星}} | Kyūshi Kasei | Fire | {{ill|South|ja|南}} | {{ill|Li|ja|離}} | style="background:purple;color:white;"| Purple |}
Despite their names, the Nine Stars are not related to celestial objects like the planets in the {{ill|Solar System|ja|太陽系}}.
== Relationships Among the Stars == The relationships among the Nine Stars follow the {{ill|five elements theory|ja|五行思想}}:
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Beneficial (Compatible) ! Neutral (Compatible) ! Harmful (Incompatible) |- ! First White Water Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Second Black Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Third Blue Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fourth Green Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fifth Yellow Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Sixth White Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Ninth Purple Fire Star | First White Water Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star |}
These relationships are used for predicting {{ill|compatibility|ja|相性}}, {{ill|fortune|ja|運勢}}, and {{ill|auspicious directions|ja|吉方位}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|I Ching|ja|易}} * {{ill|Takashima Divination|ja|高島易断}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|ja|風水}} * {{ill|Ki Astrology|ja|気学}} * {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行説}} * {{ill|Calendar Annotations|ja|暦注}}

== The Nine Stars == In Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:陰陽道]], the following table associates the Nine Stars with the {{ill|five elements|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:五行思想]], {{ill|directions|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:方位]], {{ill|eight trigrams|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:八卦]], and colors, forming the '''Nine Stars Chart''' used to predict {{ill|fortune|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:運勢]] and the {{ill|auspiciousness|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:吉凶]] of directions based on a person's birth year.
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Reading ! Five Element ! Direction ! Eight Trigram ! Color |- ! {{ill|First White Water Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:一白水星]] | Ippaku Suisei | Water | {{ill|North|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:北]] | {{ill|Kan|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:坎]] | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Second Black Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:二黒土星]] | Jikoku Dosei | Earth | Southwest | {{ill|Kun|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:坤]] | style="background:black;color:white;"| Black |- ! {{ill|Third Blue Wood Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:三碧木星]] | Sanpeki Mokusei | Wood | {{ill|East|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:東]] | {{ill|Zhen|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:震]] | style="background:blue;color:white;"| Blue |- ! {{ill|Fourth Green Wood Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:四緑木星]] | Shiroku Mokusei | Wood | Southeast | {{ill|Xun|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:巽]] | style="background:green;color:white;"| Green |- ! {{ill|Fifth Yellow Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:五黄土星]] | Goō Dosei | Earth | Center | {{ill|Tai Chi|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:太極]] | style="background:yellow;"| Yellow |- ! {{ill|Sixth White Metal Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:六白金星]] | Roppaku Kinsei | Metal | Northwest | {{ill|Qian|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:乾]] | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Seventh Red Metal Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:七赤金星]] | Shichiseki Kinsei | Metal | {{ill|West|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:西]] | {{ill|Dui|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:兌]] | style="background:red;color:white;"| Red |- ! {{ill|Eighth White Earth Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:八白土星]] | Happaku Dosei | Earth | Northeast | {{ill|Gen|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:艮]] | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Ninth Purple Fire Star|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:九紫火星]] | Kyūshi Kasei | Fire | {{ill|South|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:南]] | {{ill|Li|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:離]] | style="background:purple;color:white;"| Purple |}
Despite their names, the Nine Stars are not related to celestial objects like the planets in the {{ill|Solar System|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:太陽系]].
== Relationships Among the Stars == The relationships among the Nine Stars follow the {{ill|five elements theory|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:五行思想]]:
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Beneficial (Compatible) ! Neutral (Compatible) ! Harmful (Incompatible) |- ! First White Water Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Second Black Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Third Blue Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fourth Green Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fifth Yellow Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Sixth White Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Ninth Purple Fire Star | First White Water Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star |}
These relationships are used for predicting {{ill|compatibility|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:相性]], {{ill|fortune|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:運勢]], and {{ill|auspicious directions|en}}[[category:new pages]][[ja:吉方位]].

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Nine Celestial Stems]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nine Celestial Stems]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:九星]] [[Category:Nine Celestial Stems forks]]

{{otheruses|Nine Stars of Divination|Nine Stars in Indian Astronomy|Nine Luminaries]] {{ill|Calendar Annotations|en}}[[ja:暦注]]{{Cite source|date=December 2018]] {{color]] '''Nine Stars''' ({{ill|Nine Stars|en}}[[ja:九星]], Kyūsei) is a form of [[folk belief]] originating from ancient [[China]], consisting of nine stars: '''First White''', '''Second Black''', '''Third Blue''', '''Fourth Green''', '''Fifth Yellow''', '''Sixth White''', '''Seventh Red''', '''Eighth White''', and '''Ninth Purple'''.
The term "Nine Stars" can refer to several systems, and the one described here is specifically referred to as the "Purple White Nine Stars" or "Seven Colored Stars."
== Origin == The Nine Stars system originates from the following {{ill|magic square|en}}[[ja:魔方陣]]:
{| class="wikitable" |- |Four||Nine||Two |- |Three||Five||Seven |- |Eight||One||Six |}
In this magic square, the sum of any row, column, or diagonal equals 15. It uses the numbers 1 through 9 exactly once in a 3×3 grid. This configuration, called the '''Later Heaven Arrangement''' ({{ill|Later Heaven Arrangement|en}}[[ja:後天定位盤]]), is the only one of its kind, excluding rotations and reflections. These numbers are paired with seven colors (white, black, blue, green, yellow, red, and purple), the {{ill|five elements|en}}[[ja:五行思想]] ({{ill|wood|en}}[[ja:木]], {{ill|fire|en}}[[ja:火]], {{ill|earth|en}}[[ja:土]], {{ill|metal|en}}[[ja:金]], and {{ill|water|en}}[[ja:水]]), the {{ill|ten heavenly stems|en}}[[ja:十干]], the {{ill|twelve earthly branches|en}}[[ja:十二支]], and the {{ill|eight trigrams|en}}[[ja:八卦]] of {{ill|I Ching|en}}[[ja:易経]]. When these numbers shift positions, interpretations are added to form the "Nine Stars."
According to legend, {{ill|Yu the Great|en}}[[ja:禹]], founder of the {{ill|Xia Dynasty|en}}[[ja:夏王朝]], conceived this magic square when he encountered a sacred turtle emerging from the {{ill|Luo River|en}}[[ja:洛水]]. This magic square is called the "Writing of the Luo" ({{ill|Luo Writing|en}}[[ja:洛書]]), one of the {{ill|River Chart and Luo Writing|en}}[[ja:河図洛書]].
== The Nine Stars == In Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|en}}[[ja:陰陽道]], the following table associates the Nine Stars with the {{ill|five elements|en}}[[ja:五行思想]], {{ill|directions|en}}[[ja:方位]], {{ill|eight trigrams|en}}[[ja:八卦]], and colors, forming the '''Nine Stars Chart''' used to predict {{ill|fortune|en}}[[ja:運勢]] and the {{ill|auspiciousness|en}}[[ja:吉凶]] of directions based on a person's birth year.
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Reading ! Five Element ! Direction ! Eight Trigram ! Color |- ! {{ill|First White Water Star|en}}[[ja:一白水星]] | Ippaku Suisei | Water | {{ill|North|en}}[[ja:北]] | {{ill|Kan|en}}[[ja:坎]] | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Second Black Earth Star|en}}[[ja:二黒土星]] | Jikoku Dosei | Earth | Southwest | {{ill|Kun|en}}[[ja:坤]] | style="background:black;color:white;"| Black |- ! {{ill|Third Blue Wood Star|en}}[[ja:三碧木星]] | Sanpeki Mokusei | Wood | {{ill|East|en}}[[ja:東]] | {{ill|Zhen|en}}[[ja:震]] | style="background:blue;color:white;"| Blue |- ! {{ill|Fourth Green Wood Star|en}}[[ja:四緑木星]] | Shiroku Mokusei | Wood | Southeast | {{ill|Xun|en}}[[ja:巽]] | style="background:green;color:white;"| Green |- ! {{ill|Fifth Yellow Earth Star|en}}[[ja:五黄土星]] | Goō Dosei | Earth | Center | {{ill|Tai Chi|en}}[[ja:太極]] | style="background:yellow;"| Yellow |- ! {{ill|Sixth White Metal Star|en}}[[ja:六白金星]] | Roppaku Kinsei | Metal | Northwest | {{ill|Qian|en}}[[ja:乾]] | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Seventh Red Metal Star|en}}[[ja:七赤金星]] | Shichiseki Kinsei | Metal | {{ill|West|en}}[[ja:西]] | {{ill|Dui|en}}[[ja:兌]] | style="background:red;color:white;"| Red |- ! {{ill|Eighth White Earth Star|en}}[[ja:八白土星]] | Happaku Dosei | Earth | Northeast | {{ill|Gen|en}}[[ja:艮]] | style="background:white;"| White |- ! {{ill|Ninth Purple Fire Star|en}}[[ja:九紫火星]] | Kyūshi Kasei | Fire | {{ill|South|en}}[[ja:南]] | {{ill|Li|en}}[[ja:離]] | style="background:purple;color:white;"| Purple |}
Despite their names, the Nine Stars are not related to celestial objects like the planets in the {{ill|Solar System|en}}[[ja:太陽系]].
== Relationships Among the Stars == The relationships among the Nine Stars follow the {{ill|five elements theory|en}}[[ja:五行思想]]:
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Star ! Beneficial (Compatible) ! Neutral (Compatible) ! Harmful (Incompatible) |- ! First White Water Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Second Black Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Third Blue Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fourth Green Wood Star | First White Water Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Eighth White Earth Star |- ! Fifth Yellow Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Sixth White Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Seventh Red Metal Star | First White Water Star, Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star | Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star |- ! Eighth White Earth Star | Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star, Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | First White Water Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star |- ! Ninth Purple Fire Star | Second Black Earth Star, Third Blue Wood Star, Fourth Green Wood Star, Fifth Yellow Earth Star, Eighth White Earth Star | Ninth Purple Fire Star | First White Water Star, Sixth White Metal Star, Seventh Red Metal Star |}
These relationships are used for predicting {{ill|compatibility|en}}[[ja:相性]], {{ill|fortune|en}}[[ja:運勢]], and {{ill|auspicious directions|en}}[[ja:吉方位]].

revision.text:
{{Cite need|date=January 2020}} '''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|calendar|ja|暦}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説}} and the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}
{{Uranai-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Fortune telling]] [[Category:Divination mathematics]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Rekichū]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Calendar Annotations-qq]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Calendar Annotations-qq]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bumping for category 8]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[ja:暦注]]

Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説}} and the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.

{{Cite need|date=January 2020}} '''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|calendar|ja|暦}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説}} and the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}

{{Cite need|date=January 2020}} '''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|calendar|ja|暦}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説}} and the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}
{{Uranai-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Fortune telling]] [[Category:Divination mathematics]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Rekichū]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!----->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!--Calendar Annotations-->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!----->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!--Calendar Annotations-->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bumping for category 8]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:暦注]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:sikyokyo]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:Calendar Annotations]] [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:Calendar Annotations]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[ja:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:11]]

{{Cite need|date=January 2020}} ''''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|calendar|ja|暦}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}} and the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}
{{Uranai-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Fortune telling]] [[Category:Divination mathematics]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Rekichū]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!----->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!--Calendar Annotations-->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!----->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!--Calendar Annotations-->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bumping for category 8]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]] [[ja:暦注]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:sikyokyo]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:Calendar Annotations]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:Calendar Annotations]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[ja:Calendar Annotations]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Cite need|date=January 2020}} ''''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|calendar|ja|暦}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}} and the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}
{{Uranai-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Fortune telling]] [[Category:Divination mathematics]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Rekichū]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]][[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!--Calendar Annotations-->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]][[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]]<!--Calendar Annotations-->[[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bumping for category 8]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Calendrical Notes]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]] [[ja:暦注]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:sikyokyo]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:alendar Annotations]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:alendar Annotations]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[sn:alendar Annotations]] [[Category:11]][[sn:alendar Annotations]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Calendar Annotations]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Deprecated qq end]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Cite need|date=January 2020}} ''''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|calendar|ja|暦}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}} and the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}

== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and the Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}} and the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}

Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. {{Cite need|date=January 2020}} ''''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|calendar|ja|暦}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|directions|ja|方位}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}} and the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} and {{ill|Twelve Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日}}, {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}, {{ill|28 mansions|ja|二十八宿}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段}}. <!-- These middle and lower sections are collectively referred to as Rekichū. -->
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法}}.
== See also == {{Div col}} * {{ill|Calendar|ja|カレンダー}} * {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書}} * {{ill|Lifestyle almanac|ja|生活暦}} * {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄}} {{Div col end}}
== External links == * [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page * {{kotobank}}

Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日|1=day of the week|2=ja|3=曜日|qq=|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/days of the week|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the week|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41825}}, [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/72 pentads|qq=}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūni choku|qq=|1=Twelve Directions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404810}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sembi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|28 mansions]], {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Star Ki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekichū Gedan|qq=}}.

The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senpō|qq=}}.

'''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a [[:en:Calendar|calendar]] that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]], and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} and the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] and [[:en:Earthly Branches|Twelve Earthly Branches]] ([[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日|1=day of the week|2=ja|3=曜日|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/days of the week|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the week|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41825}}, [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/72 pentads|qq=}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūni choku|1=Twelve Directions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404810}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sembi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|28 mansions]], {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Star Ki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekichū Gedan|qq=}}. 
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senpō|qq=}}.

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:暦注]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11514953]]
Wikidata name: rekichū
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenced|date=January 2020|qq=}}
'''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a [[:en:Calendar|calendar]] that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]], and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} and the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] and [[:en:Earthly Branches|Twelve Earthly Branches]] ([[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日|1=day of the week|2=ja|3=曜日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/days of the week|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the week|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41825}}, [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/72 pentads|qq=}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūni choku|1=Twelve Directions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404810}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sembi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|28 mansions]], {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Star Ki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekichū Gedan|qq=}}. 
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senpō|qq=}}.
== See also ==
{{Div col|qq=}}
* [[:en:Calendar (stationery)|Calendar]]
* {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous works|qq=}}
* [[:en:Almanac|Lifestyle almanac]]
* {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{Div col end|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:暦注]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11514953]]
Wikidata name: rekichū
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenced|date=January 2020|qq=}}
'''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a [[:en:Calendar|calendar]] that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]], and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} and the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] and [[:en:Earthly Branches|Twelve Earthly Branches]] ([[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日|1=day of the week|2=ja|3=曜日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/days of the week|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the week|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41825}}, [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/72 pentads|qq=}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūni choku|1=Twelve Directions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404810}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sembi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|28 mansions]], {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Star Ki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekichū Gedan|qq=}}. 
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senpō|qq=}}.
== See also ==
{{Div col|qq=}}
* [[:en:Calendar (stationery)|Calendar]]
* {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous works|qq=}}
* [[:en:Almanac|Lifestyle almanac]]
* {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{Div col end|qq=}}
== External links ==
* [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page
* {{kotobank|qq=}}
{{Uranai-stub|qq=}}
{{Normdaten|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|暦注|version=102799126|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Unreferenced|date=January 2020|qq=}}
'''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a [[:en:Calendar|calendar]] that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]], and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} and the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] and [[:en:Earthly Branches|Twelve Earthly Branches]] ([[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]]). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日|1=day of the week|2=ja|3=曜日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/days of the week|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the week|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41825}}, [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/72 pentads|qq=}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūni choku|1=Twelve Directions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404810}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sembi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|28 mansions]], {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Star Ki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekichū Gedan|qq=}}. 
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senpō|qq=}}.
== See also ==
{{Div col|qq=}}
* [[:en:Calendar (stationery)|Calendar]]
* {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous works|qq=}}
* [[:en:Almanac|Lifestyle almanac]]
* {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{Div col end|qq=}}
== External links ==
* [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page
* {{kotobank|qq=}}
{{Uranai-stub|qq=}}
{{Normdaten|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:暦注]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:暦注]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:暦注]]

{{Unreferenced|date=January 2020|qq=}}
'''Rekichū''' (暦注) refers to annotations in a {{ill|Calendar|en|Calendar|WD=Q12132|lt=calendar}} that indicate the auspiciousness (吉凶) of times, {{ill|Cardinal direction|en|Cardinal direction|WD=Q23718|lt=Five directions}}, and other elements related to the fortune (運勢) of particular days.
== Overview ==
Most Rekichū are based on the principles of {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} and the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems}} and {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=Twelve Earthly Branches}} ({{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle}}). Generally, calendars list scientific and astronomical details such as dates, {{ill|days of the week|ja|曜日|1=day of the week|2=ja|3=曜日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/days of the week|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the week|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41825}}, {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=solar terms}}, and {{ill|72 pentads|ja|七十二候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/72 pentads|qq=}} in the top section. The middle section typically features {{ill|Jūni choku|ja|十二直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūni choku|1=Twelve Directions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404810}}, while the lower section contains items like {{ill|Sembi|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sembi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=28 mansions}}, {{ill|Nine Star Ki|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Star Ki|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rekichū Gedan|ja|暦注下段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekichū Gedan|qq=}}. 
{{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} often appears under the date and is also considered part of Rekichū.
The method used to determine the dates in Rekichū is called {{ill|Senpō|ja|撰日法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senpō|qq=}}.
== See also ==
{{Div col|qq=}}
* [[:en:Calendar (stationery)|Calendar]]
* {{ill|Miscellaneous works|ja|雑書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous works|qq=}}
* {{ill|Almanac|en|Almanac|WD=Q171341|lt=Lifestyle almanac}}
* {{ill|Higara|ja|日柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{Div col end|qq=}}
== External links ==
* [https://koyomi8.com/directjp.cgi?https://koyomi8.com/sub/rekicyuu.html Rekichū Calculation] - Koyomi no Page
* {{kotobank|qq=}}
{{Uranai-stub|qq=}}
{{Normdaten|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|暦注|version=102799126|comment=Imported full ja history}}
